title: AI Privacy and Data Security

modules.title: How AI Uses Personal Data
modules.sub: Tracing how AI systems collect and process your information
modules.description: Learners map the data lifecycle within AI systems, from collection and storage to analysis and inference. They examine real-world examples of how platforms and models extract personal signals from everyday interactions. By the end, learners can articulate exactly where and how their data enters AI pipelines.

modules.title: Privacy Risks and Threats
modules.sub: Identifying the ways AI can expose or exploit personal information
modules.description: Learners assess a range of privacy threats—including data breaches, re-identification attacks, and model inversion—that emerge specifically from AI-driven systems. They evaluate case studies to recognize which threat vectors are most relevant to their professional context. This module builds a threat-modeling mindset grounded in real AI risk scenarios.

modules.title: Protecting Your Digital Footprint
modules.sub: Applying practical strategies to minimize AI-driven data exposure
modules.description: Learners select and apply concrete privacy-preserving techniques, such as data minimization, anonymization tools, and permission auditing, to reduce their exposure across AI-powered services. They work through decision frameworks to evaluate trade-offs between convenience and privacy in professional settings. The module culminates in a personal digital footprint audit and an action plan.

modules.title: AI Governance and Regulation
modules.sub: Navigating the laws and frameworks shaping responsible AI data use
modules.description: Learners survey the global regulatory landscape—including GDPR, CCPA, and emerging AI-specific legislation—and analyze how these frameworks impose obligations on AI developers and deployers. They evaluate compliance gaps and consider how governance standards translate into organizational policy decisions. Learners leave with the ability to assess regulatory risk and advocate for responsible AI data practices in their organizations.

synopsis: Explore how AI systems collect, process, and potentially misuse personal information in our digital lives. Master practical strategies to protect your privacy while understanding emerging laws and regulations governing AI development.
